author: serenita_io
content:
Even after reading through the PR, 404 seems more consistent with the current Beacon API endpoints and more appropriate to me for this 🤷
I voted for 404, right now the vote is 5:3 in favor of 204. Vero can handle both cases just fine, that's no problem

author: nflaig
content: the consistency argument is valid, but I would like to avoid this argument, it means we have to carry forward bad decision from the past for the sake of being consistent which I do not like. but curious, why do you think it's more appropriate? I feel like using 404 in `getBlocksV2` and other apis was a mistake

author: serenita_io
content:
Because the VC requests data for a slot, and that data doesn't exist, in other words, is not found. For that reason 404 seems more appropriate to me.

204 seems more appropriate for POST/PUT requests where the server wants to indicate it has processed the request successfully but there is no need to return any data.

author: nflaig
content:
so regarding your first sentence
> The 4xx (Client Error) class of status code indicates that the client seems to have erred
this is not a mistake of the client, so using 4xx is just not appropriate per rfc9110

and your second sentence
> there is no need to return any data
that's exactly the case here, there is no block for slot, so there no need for the ptc to vote and no need to return any data

return errors for normal behavior has been issues in the past that are also operational, all minor but still, to name a few, emitted warning logs because blocks are missed (which is normal), wrong error tracking on various software (not gonna call out names here), and also see my comment on the github PR, I literally got an alert due to this because the AI thought there is a problem with the ptc voting due to all the 404s

author: nflaig
content: something worth pointing out, the [getHeader](https://ethereum.github.io/builder-specs/#/Builder/getHeader) today uses 204 if there is no header/bid available, so even the consistency argument doesn't hold throughout

author: serenita_io
content:
> this is not a mistake of the client, so using 4xx is just not appropriate per rfc9110

Neither is requesting a missed block using `getBlockV2` a client mistake. Besides the "route not implemented" argument mentioned in the PR, this behavior has also bitten me in the past when requesting blocks for an older slot range which had already been pruned away by the beacon node -> those requests returned 404 even though there were blocks proposed in those slots.

In the interest of not repeating mistakes from the past despite breaking a bit of consistency, the 204 would be more palateable to me if the endpoint path would be generic and the slot is requested via a query parameter, so `GET /eth/v1/validator/payload_attestation_data?slot=1234`. That avoids the 404 route not implemented ambiguity and also makes the  endpoint consistent with the endpoint used for producing `AttestationData` (which also takes a `slot` query param).

author: nflaig
content: I think using `query` for a required parameter instead of `path` is another mistake from the past 😅

author: nflaig
content: but I would have to read up on the rfc on when it's more appropriate to use path vs. query

author: nflaig
content:
so after reading through rfc 3986 a bit I don't see a strong argument for using `path` here, maybe even the opposite, it seems like `path` should be used for stored sub-resources while `query` should be favored for on demand computation, so in this case `query` would be correct. Also as you noted, both `produceSyncCommitteeContribution` and `produceAttestationData` have slot as a required query param. moving it to `query` would also resolve the argument from <@680234424037670912> to use 404

so I would suggest the following
- we merge the 204 status response PR https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/612
- and do a follow up that changes the route to `GET /eth/v1/validator/payload_attestation_data?slot={slot}`

that should resolve the discussion unless I missed something, <@737740104281489531> can you open that route change PR, I think we should do this now since we are changing it anyways and are not testing mixed client setups so it shouldn't break anything

author: serenita_io
content: Here's the followup PR, there shouldn't be a conflict with 612 - https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/626

author: jameshe5018
content: <@346498651553660928> sent a friend request, wanted to send a message about commit boost ( i'm from the prysm team and accidently broke connection by default)

author: yorickdowne
content: It'd be very nice to have this exposed via keymanager in some way. Right now I'm abstracting per-key setup via keymanager, same code for all VCs. Needing to understand the config file layout for every client is not something I'm looking forward to

author: jvranek
terencechain
content:
Agree this belongs in the keymanager API. The per-key object in my PR `(fee_recipient, gas_limit, graffiti, builder{...})` is shaped to be exactly what such an endpoint would POST, so folding the existing feerecipient/gas_limit endpoints into one per-key config call is the right move, and it gives Yorick the cross-client uniformity that `--proposer-settings-url` (a prysm flag) can't.

Just keep the layers separate: keymanager (manager→VC), Potuz's 625 (VC→BN), builder-specs #138 (BN→builder) can share the object but shouldn't block each other.

Can someone in the keymanager-API repo pick up that PR? Happy to keep the prysm object aligned and to review

author: serenita_io
content:
I posted my feedback on #625 in the PR - https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/625#issuecomment-4928025035 .

TLDR:
1) I'm strongly in favor of doing this the Keymanager way instead of every CL having their own proprietary file format.
2) Do we actually expect these very advanced per-key per-builder config options to be widely used? If not, there should be room for simplification.
